title: CheckMyCoach: A Calibration Pipeline for LLM Uncertainty Expression
keyTakeaways: CheckMyCoach is a four-stage calibration pipeline (Detection → Diagnosis → Correction → Validation) that automatically identifies and rewrites Pseudo-precise and Overconfident LLM outputs.
The pipeline improved Overconfident outputs by +0.75 UCS points and Pseudo-precise outputs by +0.17 UCS points without degrading already-Calibrated outputs (Δ = 0.00).
The validation stage (M4) enforces four sanity checks and falls back to the original output on failure, prioritizing 'do no harm' over maximum correction coverage.
At ~$0.001 per correction via GPT-4o-mini, the pipeline is cost-effective for real-time deployment in AI advisory systems.
hciRelevance: Tests whether calibration is a trainable skill — if trust can be repaired through automated correction, then calibration is a design problem, not a user education problem.

faq.q: What failure types does CheckMyCoach diagnose?
faq.a: Three types, priority-ordered: (1) Context Mismatch — contradictory features in the output, (2) Template Dominance — superiority claims without evidence, (3) Cue Leakage — pseudo-precise numbers without evidence grounding.

faq.q: Does CheckMyCoach ever make outputs worse?
faq.a: In 3 of 19 scorable cases, corrections lowered the UCS score. These cases are a focus of ongoing improvement. The validation stage (M4) catches basic errors (truncation, unchanged text, increased assertion count) and falls back to the original output.

faq.q: Is it worth building a four-stage system for 6.3% of outputs?
faq.a: Yes — Pseudo-precise outputs cluster in the most safety-critical scenarios (23.7% on mixed-evidence questions), and at 10 statements per session, users face ~48% cumulative exposure. In safety-critical domains, individual undetected errors carry high costs.

citation.apa: Guo, M. (2026). From Precision Illusion to Calibration Intervention: A Framework for Evaluating and Correcting LLM Uncertainty Expression in Safety-Critical Advice. https://gbx-max1220.github.io/maxguo.dev/research/building-trustworthy-ai/checkmycoach/
citation.chicago: Guo, Max. "From Precision Illusion to Calibration Intervention." 2026. https://gbx-max1220.github.io/maxguo.dev/research/building-trustworthy-ai/checkmycoach/.
citation.mla: Guo, Max. "From Precision Illusion to Calibration Intervention." 2026, https://gbx-max1220.github.io/maxguo.dev/research/building-trustworthy-ai/checkmycoach/.

reproducible.env: Python 3.12 with requirements.txt; OpenRouter API key for M3 (GPT-4o-mini); fallback mode available when API is offline
reproducible.quickStart: git clone ... && cd CheckMyCoach && pip install -r requirements.txt && python calibration_agent/e2e_test.py

changelog.event: Paper complete (8.5/9 sections). Discussion expanded to ~985 words. Human Evaluation skeleton in place.
44 tests (41 unit + 3 e2e) all passing. Baseline comparison (24 cases) complete.
M1-M4 pipeline complete with UCS Engine integration
